description:
Sixteen-plus source and test files sit at 285-299 lines, six of them at exactly 299 — one line under the hard pre-commit cap. The cap is doing its job: it is a proxy for 'this file holds more than one cohesive idea.' These files cannot absorb a single new line, which means the next feature touching them forces a rushed split under deadline pressure rather than a considered one now.

Goal: split each along a genuine logical seam (a self-contained type, a cohesive group of helpers, a distinct command, a test theme) into a well-named sibling module — not arbitrary line-count chopping. Public API and behavior unchanged; re-export moved items so callers and lib.rs stay stable. All tests pass, clippy clean, 100% coverage, every file under 300.
title: Decompose files near the 300-line cap into logical modules
